codeReview.summary: Reviewed mission artifacts, worker transcript skeleton, and repository history for crash-resilience. The feature behavior is covered by the implemented git sync script (commit d63a438) plus validation tests and operational checks captured in the handoff (commit fbca445 as handoff commit). I found no blocking defects, but a few tests are weaker than their names imply.

issues.description: `test_data_persisted_after_insert` claims crash-survival validation but only performs INSERT and SELECT without simulating a crash/restart. This weakens regression coverage for the stated requirement "partial data survives after simulated crash".

`test_bd_list_status_flag_works` verifies command availability but does not simulate a crash/restart before checking discoverability. It does not directly test the "after crash" condition in expected behavior.

`test_gt_doctor_runs` asserts output contains "passed" but does not require a zero exit code, so it can pass even when `gt doctor --fix` reports failures. This is less strict than expected behavior wording "runs without error".

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission AGENTS boundary text says custom research code must be under `research/` only, but validator scope and accepted feature work include `tests/runner/`. This rule is currently ambiguous for workers adding mission tests.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /home/ubuntu/.factory/missions/87f1dbde-093a-4612-97da-91c8bec9fe63/AGENTS.md:6 states `research/`-only, while lines 40-41 explicitly scope validators to `tests/runner/` and `tests/analysis/`; this feature adds `tests/runner/test_crash_resilience.py`.

summary: Pass with non-blocking test-quality gaps. Core crash-resilience artifacts are present in repository history and handoff evidence, but several new tests should more directly exercise crash/restart conditions to harden regression coverage.
